title: 2027 Global Pluralism Award: Apply or Nominate Outstanding Leaders and Organizations Advancing Inclusive Societies Worldwide
summary: The 2027 Global Pluralism Award is now accepting submissions from individuals, organizations, governments, and businesses that are making remarkable contributions toward building more inclusive societies around the world. Presented by the Global Centre for Pluralism, this prestigious international award recognizes and celebrates exceptional achievements in promoting pluralism, diversity, inclusion, equality, and social cohesion. The award serves as a global platform for highlighting innovative and courageous efforts that help societies embrace diversity while ensuring that all people can participate fully and equally in social, political, economic, and cultural life. Whether you are an individual championing inclusion, an organization advocating for marginalized communities, a government implementing transformative policies, or a business fostering diversity and belonging, the Global Pluralism Award offers an opportunity for international recognition of your impact. What is the Global Pluralism Award? The Global Pluralism Award is an international recognition program that honors pluralism in action. Presented every two years, the award celebrates inspiring initiatives and sustained efforts that contribute to creating societies where diversity is not merely tolerated but actively valued and protected. The award acknowledges individuals and institutions that have demonstrated leadership, innovation, resilience, and commitment in advancing inclusion and fostering peaceful coexistence among diverse communities. Through this initiative, the Global Centre for Pluralism aims to elevate successful models of pluralism from around the world, encourage learning across borders, and inspire others to take action toward more equitable and inclusive societies. Understanding Pluralism Pluralism is a positive response to diversity. It goes beyond simply recognizing differences among people. Pluralism involves: Respecting diverse identities, beliefs, cultures, and perspectives. Creating opportunities for all individuals and communities to participate meaningfully in society. Promoting equality and inclusion. Addressing barriers that prevent people from accessing opportunities and rights. Encouraging dialogue, cooperation, and mutual understanding across differences. Building institutions and systems that support fairness, belonging, and social cohesion. The Global Pluralism Award recognizes those who transform these principles into practical action and lasting impact. Who Can Apply or Be Nominated? The award welcomes submissions from a broad range of actors working to advance pluralism, including: Individuals Non-governmental organizations Community-based organizations Civil society groups Governments and public institutions Private-sector businesses Social enterprises Coalitions and networks Candidates may either apply directly or be nominated by others who recognize their contributions to advancing inclusive societies. Why the Global Pluralism Award Matters Around the world, societies continue to face challenges related to exclusion, discrimination, inequality, conflict, and social division. The Global Pluralism Award shines a spotlight on practical solutions and inspiring leadership that demonstrate how diversity can become a source of strength rather than division. Award recipients become part of a growing international network of leaders and organizations committed to promoting inclusion and social transformation. The award helps: Recognize impactful work on a global stage. Increase visibility for successful initiatives. Inspire replication of effective approaches. Encourage cross-border learning and collaboration. Promote dialogue on diversity and inclusion. Strengthen efforts to build peaceful and resilient societies. Selection Criteria Candidates will be evaluated based on their demonstrated commitment to advancing pluralism and creating inclusive societies. Successful submissions typically show: Exceptional achievements in promoting pluralism. Sustained impact over time. Innovative approaches to addressing exclusion and inequality. Measurable outcomes and positive societal change. Leadership and courage in challenging environments. Potential to inspire others globally. An independent international jury will assess candidates through a rigorous multi-stage evaluation process. 2027 Global Pluralism Award Selection Process The selection process follows several stages designed to ensure fairness, transparency, and thorough evaluation. August 2026 – Submission Processing The Global Centre for Pluralism Secretariat receives and reviews submissions. Applications undergo initial screening and processing. December 2026 – Selection of Shortlist Shortlisted candidates are identified. Selected applicants may be required to provide additional supporting documentation. February 2027 – Jury Review An independent international jury of experts reviews shortlisted applications. Finalists are selected based on demonstrated excellence and impact. April 2027 – Site Visits The Centre may conduct in-person visits to selected finalists. Site visits form part of the due diligence and verification process. Fall 2027 – Public Announcement Award winners are publicly announced. Awareness campaigns and media activities follow in participating countries. November 2027 – Award Ceremony Three winners are officially recognized. Up to seven honorable mention recipients may also be celebrated during the ceremony. Award Recognition The Global Pluralism Award recognizes outstanding leaders and institutions whose work is helping transform societies. For each award cycle: Three winners are selected. Up to seven honorable mention recipients may also be recognized. Awardees receive international visibility and recognition. Winners join a distinguished network of pluralism leaders and changemakers. Examples of Previous Award Winners The 2025 Global Pluralism Award honored several remarkable organizations advancing inclusion and justice. A Land for All A joint Israeli-Palestinian political initiative promoting a shared vision rooted in dignity, equality, belonging, and coexistence for both peoples. Colombia Diversa An organization advancing justice for LGBTIQ+ communities through strategic litigation and dialogue with diverse stakeholders, including religious leaders. Southern Africa Litigation Centre An organization leveraging landmark legal actions across multiple African countries to dismantle barriers to legal, social, environmental, and human rights justice. These examples demonstrate the broad range of initiatives that can qualify for recognition under the award. Global Impact of the Award Since its establishment, the Global Pluralism Award has generated significant international engagement. Its achievements include: Multiple award cycles recognizing global excellence. Hundreds of submissions from around the world. Numerous laureates celebrated for advancing inclusion. Representation from countries across diverse regions and contexts. The award continues to strengthen global awareness of pluralism and its role in creating equitable and peaceful societies. How to Submit an Application or Nomination Interested candidates can submit an application directly or nominate deserving individuals and organizations through the official Global Pluralism Award platform. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the submission guidelines, eligibility requirements, selection criteria, and application instructions before submitting their materials. Strong applications should clearly demonstrate: The challenge being addressed. Actions taken to advance pluralism. Evidence of impact and results. Sustainability of the initiative. Lessons learned and broader relevance. Deadline Applications and nominations are currently being accepted for the 2027 Global Pluralism Award. Interested candidates should submit their applications as early as possible and monitor the official award website for any submission deadlines, updates, and additional guidance. APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The 2027 Global Pluralism Award represents a unique opportunity for individuals, organizations, governments, and businesses working to build more inclusive societies to gain international recognition for their efforts. At a time when societies around the world are navigating increasing diversity and complex social challenges, the award highlights the importance of leadership, innovation, and collaboration in creating communities where everyone can thrive. If you or someone you know is making a meaningful contribution toward inclusion, equality, and respect for diversity, consider applying or submitting a nomination for the 2027 Global Pluralism Award. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Award Opportunities on OFY here . The post 2027 Global Pluralism Award: Apply or Nominate Outstanding Leaders and Organizations Advancing Inclusive Societies Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Kingdom Research and Innovation Policy Fellowships 2026: Up to £280,000 Funding to Bridge Research and Policy for National and Global Impact
summary: Applications Open for the United Kingdom Research and Innovation Policy Fellowships 2026 The United Kingdom Research and Innovation (UKRI) Policy Fellowships 2026 present an exceptional opportunity for researchers and academics seeking to influence public policy while expanding their professional experience beyond traditional academic environments. Designed to strengthen connections between research and policymaking, these prestigious fellowships enable participants to transfer knowledge, skills, and expertise across sectors while addressing some of the most pressing national and global challenges. The fellowship programme aims to increase collaboration between academia and policy institutions by creating greater sector mobility, often referred to as sector porosity. Successful applicants will have the opportunity to work directly within policy environments, helping to shape evidence-based decision-making while gaining valuable insights into how policy is developed and implemented. With funding of up to £280,000 available and fellowships lasting 18 months, this programme represents one of the most significant opportunities for researchers interested in making a meaningful impact on society through policy engagement. About the United Kingdom Research and Innovation Policy Fellowships The fellowship programme is funded by United Kingdom Research and Innovation (UKRI) and supported by its research councils, including: Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council (BBSRC) Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC) Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC) Medical Research Council (MRC) Natural Environment Research Council (NERC) Science and Technology Facilities Council (STFC) The programme is specifically designed to facilitate the movement of expertise between research institutions and policy organisations, ensuring that high-quality research informs policy decisions and that researchers gain a deeper understanding of policy challenges. Fellowship Options Available Applicants may apply under one of the following fellowship pathways: 1. Core Policy Fellowships Core Policy Fellows will spend 18 months embedded within policy environments, contributing research expertise to support policy development and implementation. Funding Available: Up to £180,000 Full Economic Cost (FEC) UKRI funds 80% of the Full Economic Cost 2. What Works Innovation Fellowships These fellowships focus on innovation, evidence-based policymaking, and the implementation of effective solutions to public challenges. Funding Available: Up to £220,000 Full Economic Cost (FEC) UKRI funds 80% of the Full Economic Cost 3. Natural Hazards and Resilience Fellowships These fellowships focus on improving resilience and policy responses related to natural hazards and environmental risks. Funding Available: Up to £280,000 Full Economic Cost (FEC) UKRI funds 80% of the Full Economic Cost Objectives of the Fellowship Programme The UKRI Policy Fellowships seek to achieve several important goals: Strengthening Research-Policy Connections The programme creates stronger links between researchers and policymakers, ensuring that policy decisions are informed by the latest evidence and expertise. Addressing National and Global Challenges Fellows will contribute to policy discussions and solutions relating to critical societal issues affecting the United Kingdom and the wider world. Developing Professional Skills Participants will gain valuable experience in: Policy development Government engagement Strategic decision-making Stakeholder management Evidence synthesis Public sector collaboration Policy communication Promoting Sector Mobility The fellowships encourage movement between academic and policy sectors, creating professionals who can effectively operate across both environments. Benefits of the UKRI Policy Fellowships 2026 Successful fellows will receive a wide range of benefits, including: Financial Support Up to £280,000 in project funding depending on fellowship type UKRI support covering 80% of the Full Economic Cost Professional Development Direct experience within policy settings Exposure to policymaking processes Opportunities to work alongside policy leaders and decision-makers Enhanced leadership and strategic skills Research Impact Opportunity to apply research expertise to real-world challenges Increased visibility and influence of research findings Ability to contribute directly to evidence-informed policy Networking Opportunities Access to policy professionals Collaboration with government and public sector stakeholders Connections across academic and policy communities Career Advancement Enhanced career prospects within academia, government, think tanks, and policy organisations Development of highly sought-after interdisciplinary skills Fellowship Duration Successful applicants will undertake an 18-month fellowship, allowing sufficient time to: Build meaningful policy relationships Contribute to policy initiatives Apply research expertise effectively Develop long-term professional networks Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the following timeline: Application Opening Date 9 June 2026 (9:00 AM UK Time) Webinar 25 June 2026 Application Deadline 10 September 2026 (4:00 PM UK Time) Shortlisting Period October to November 2026 Interview Invitations December 2026 Interviews January 2027 Decision Notification February 2027 Fellowship Start Date 1 May 2027 Why This Fellowship Matters Modern policy challenges increasingly require evidence-based solutions. Whether addressing environmental resilience, educational outcomes, healthcare systems, innovation strategies, or technological advancement, policymakers depend on high-quality research to make informed decisions. The UKRI Policy Fellowships provide a unique mechanism for integrating research expertise directly into policymaking environments. Fellows become key contributors to policy development while gaining invaluable experience that strengthens both their careers and the wider research ecosystem. This fellowship therefore represents an outstanding opportunity for researchers who want their expertise to create tangible societal impact while developing new professional capabilities. Application Process Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official UKRI funding portal before the deadline. Prospective applicants are encouraged to: Review all eligibility requirements carefully. Identify the fellowship pathway most aligned with their expertise. Attend the programme webinar on 25 June 2026. Prepare a strong application demonstrating both research excellence and policy relevance. Submit their application before 10 September 2026 at 4:00 PM UK time. APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The United Kingdom Research and Innovation Policy Fellowships 2026 offer a rare opportunity for researchers to directly influence policy while expanding their professional horizons. With funding of up to £280,000, an 18-month placement period, and the chance to contribute to solutions for major societal challenges, the programme is ideal for individuals seeking to bridge the gap between research and policymaking. Researchers interested in increasing the real-world impact of their work should strongly consider applying before the application deadline of 10 September 2026. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post United Kingdom Research and Innovation Policy Fellowships 2026: Up to £280,000 Funding to Bridge Research and Policy for National and Global Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNODC YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board 2026–2028: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders Advancing Integrity, Ethics, and Anti-Corruption
summary: The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) has officially opened applications for the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board 2026–2028, inviting passionate and committed young people from around the world to play a leading role in promoting integrity, ethics, accountability, and anti-corruption efforts at local, regional, and global levels. Established under the Global Resource for Anti-Corruption Education and Youth Empowerment (GRACE) Initiative, the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board provides a unique platform for young people to contribute directly to the development and implementation of youth-focused anti-corruption initiatives while working alongside UNODC experts and global stakeholders. This opportunity recognizes young people not merely as beneficiaries of anti-corruption efforts, but as partners, innovators, leaders, and changemakers capable of driving meaningful action and strengthening integrity in their communities and beyond. About the UNODC GRACE Initiative The Global Resource for Anti-Corruption Education and Youth Empowerment (GRACE) Initiative is a flagship programme of the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime that seeks to empower young people and educational institutions to build cultures of integrity, transparency, accountability, and ethical leadership. The initiative supports efforts to: Promote anti-corruption education. Strengthen ethical leadership. Foster civic responsibility. Encourage youth participation in governance. Build awareness about corruption and its impacts. Support community-driven solutions to integrity challenges. Advance sustainable development through transparency and accountability. Through the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board, the GRACE Initiative places young people at the center of anti-corruption efforts under the guiding principle: “Nothing about us without us.” What is the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board? The YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board is a global advisory body composed of young people from diverse backgrounds, regions, and experiences who provide strategic guidance and support to UNODC s youth engagement and anti-corruption initiatives. The Board serves as a dedicated platform where young leaders can: Share perspectives and ideas. Influence youth engagement strategies. Promote anti-corruption awareness. Contribute to policy discussions. Support youth-led initiatives. Strengthen community participation. Help shape future integrity programmes. Board members work as co-leading partners with UNODC and the GRACE Initiative team to ensure that youth voices are meaningfully integrated into anti-corruption programming and decision-making processes. Responsibilities of YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board Members Selected members will play an active role in supporting UNODC s anti-corruption agenda and youth empowerment efforts. Core responsibilities include: 1. Advising UNODC on Youth Engagement Board members will provide recommendations and insights on how to engage young people effectively in anti-corruption initiatives and integrity-building programmes. 2. Contributing to Good Practices Participants will help develop innovative and youth-centered approaches to integrity, ethics, transparency, and accountability. 3. Supporting Campaigns and Outreach Activities Members will contribute to: Awareness campaigns. Regional initiatives. Youth workshops. Consultations. Educational programmes. Community engagement activities. 4. Promoting Integrity Within Communities Board members are expected to champion anti-corruption values within their own communities and networks. 5. Creating Opportunities for Youth Participation Participants will help identify and create pathways for young people to engage in anti-corruption action and civic participation. Why This Opportunity Matters Corruption remains one of the greatest barriers to sustainable development, social justice, good governance, and economic progress worldwide. Young people are increasingly recognized as powerful agents of change capable of challenging corruption and promoting accountability. Through the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board, UNODC aims to: Amplify youth voices. Support youth-led innovation. Encourage civic participation. Build leadership capacity. Strengthen ethical governance. Expand global youth networks focused on integrity and transparency. The Board offers a rare opportunity for young leaders to directly contribute to international anti-corruption efforts while gaining valuable experience in global governance and advocacy. Who Should Apply? UNODC encourages applications from motivated young people who are passionate about integrity, ethics, accountability, and social change. Applicants do not need prior experience working with the United Nations. The programme is particularly interested in young people involved in: Anti-corruption initiatives. Ethics and integrity promotion. Peacebuilding. Rule of law advocacy. Civic engagement. Youth participation programmes. Community action projects. Governance initiatives. Social justice movements. Youth-led networks and organizations. Applicants from marginalized, underrepresented, and diverse communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible, applicants must: Age Requirement Be between 18 and 29 years old at the time of application. Language Requirement Be able to communicate effectively in English. All Board activities, communications, meetings, and documents are conducted in English. Interest Requirement Applicants should demonstrate interest, involvement, or commitment to areas such as: Integrity. Ethics. Anti-corruption. Rule of law. Justice. Civic engagement. Community leadership. Youth empowerment. Board Term and Commitment Selected members will serve: Initial Appointment Minimum of one year. Overall Mandate Two-year mandate (2026–2028). Renewal Opportunity The mandate may be renewed once for an additional period of up to two years. Maximum Service Period Up to four years total service. Monthly Time Commitment Board members should dedicate approximately: Three days per month. Activities may include: Participating in meetings. Reviewing documents. Supporting campaigns. Contributing to consultations. Engaging in community outreach. Assisting with youth-focused initiatives. Is the Position Paid? Participation on the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board is entirely voluntary . Board members: Do not receive financial compensation. Serve in a volunteer capacity. Gain valuable leadership, networking, advocacy, and international engagement opportunities. Application Requirements Applicants must complete the official application form and submit the following materials: Curriculum Vitae (CV) A current CV highlighting educational background, leadership experience, community engagement, and relevant activities. Motivation Letter Applicants must submit: One-page motivation letter. Maximum 500 words (excluding spaces). The letter should explain: Why they wish to join the Board. Their commitment to integrity and anti-corruption. How they can contribute to the Board s work. Short Video Submission Applicants must record and submit: A video of up to 90 seconds. The video should introduce the applicant and demonstrate motivation, passion, and commitment to anti-corruption and youth engagement. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Motivation and commitment. Availability. Relevant experience or demonstrated interest. Community engagement. Youth leadership potential. Diversity considerations. Geographic representation. Community reach and influence. Ability to contribute meaningfully to anti-corruption efforts. Special consideration will be given to applicants from countries that have not previously been represented on the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board. Previously Represented Countries Current and former YouthLED Board members have come from countries including: Argentina, Bangladesh, Benin, Brazil, Botswana, Burkina Faso, Cambodia, China, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Egypt, Ethiopia, Georgia, Germany, Ghana, India, Italy, Japan, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Republic of Korea, Lebanon, Liberia, Mongolia, Mexico, Morocco, Nepal, Nigeria, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Portugal, Rwanda, El Salvador, South Africa, Sudan, Trinidad and Tobago, Togo, Tunisia, Uganda, United States of America, Uzbekistan, Vietnam, and Zimbabwe. Application Timeline Application Deadline 14 August 2026 Review Period 15 August – 25 September 2026 During this phase, applications will be reviewed and shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews. Notification of Selected Candidates 5 October 2026 Start of New Board Members Term 30 October 2026 APPLY HERE See the Terms of Reference and Code of Conduct Benefits of Joining the YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board Selected members will gain: Direct engagement with UNODC. Global leadership experience. International visibility. Opportunities to shape anti-corruption initiatives. Access to global youth networks. Experience in policy discussions and consultations. Capacity-building and professional development opportunities. Opportunities to support global awareness campaigns. Enhanced advocacy and communication skills. Exposure to international governance processes. Visit HERE to learn more about the UNODC YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board 2026–2028. Conclusion The UNODC YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board 2026–2028 offers a remarkable opportunity for young leaders who are passionate about integrity, ethics, accountability, and anti-corruption. Through this unique platform, selected members will work alongside the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime to strengthen youth participation in global anti-corruption efforts, contribute innovative ideas, and inspire action within their communities. For young changemakers seeking to expand their leadership, influence international conversations, and help build a more transparent and accountable future, this opportunity provides an exceptional pathway to make a meaningful and lasting impact on a global scale. Discover more global Fellowship , Leadership Development and UN opportunities on Opportunities For Youth (OFY). The post UNODC YouthLED Integrity Advisory Board 2026–2028: Global Opportunity for Young Leaders Advancing Integrity, Ethics, and Anti-Corruption appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FoodHaus Fellowship for Action 2026–2027: Fully Immersive 9-Month Global Fellowship Empowering Changemakers to Transform Food Systems
summary: Applications are now open for the second cohort of the FoodHaus Fellowship for Action, a transformative 9-month fellowship designed for individuals committed to creating a more just, sustainable, and resilient food system. The program brings together passionate changemakers from diverse professional backgrounds and equips them with the knowledge, leadership skills, networks, and practical tools needed to turn innovative ideas into meaningful action. Hosted by FoodHaus, the Fellowship for Action offers participants a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of food systems while developing real-world projects and interventions that address pressing challenges in food production, distribution, accessibility, sustainability, and community well-being. Whether you are a farmer, chef, scientist, artist, educator, social entrepreneur, policymaker, activist, social worker, development professional, or simply someone passionate about food systems transformation, this fellowship provides a supportive environment to learn, collaborate, and lead impactful change. Early Bird and Scholarship Deadline Application Deadline for Early Bird Registration and Scholarship Consideration: July 1, 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of securing available scholarship support. About the FoodHaus Fellowship for Action The FoodHaus Fellowship for Action is specifically designed for individuals who believe that food can serve as a powerful catalyst for social, environmental, and economic transformation. Over a period of nine months, fellows will participate in an immersive learning journey that combines: Systems thinking Collaborative leadership development Personal growth and self-awareness Practical project design and implementation Community engagement International peer learning The fellowship recognizes that creating meaningful change within food systems requires more than technical knowledge. It also demands strong leadership, relationship-building skills, personal resilience, and the ability to work collaboratively across sectors and cultures. Through a carefully structured curriculum, participants will explore food systems challenges, identify leverage points for change, and design initiatives capable of generating lasting impact within their communities, organizations, or regions. Program Duration The fellowship runs from: October 2026 to August 2027 During this period, fellows will engage in a variety of online and in-person learning experiences that support both personal and professional development. What Fellows Will Experience 1. Weekly Online Learning Sessions Participants will take part in regular virtual sessions throughout the fellowship period. These sessions provide opportunities to: Explore complex food system challenges Learn systems-thinking methodologies Participate in collaborative discussions Develop leadership capabilities Share experiences with fellow changemakers Receive guidance from experts and mentors The online format allows participants from different parts of the world to engage consistently while maintaining their existing professional and community commitments. 2. Personal Coaching Support One of the distinctive features of the fellowship is individualized coaching. Participants will receive personal coaching that helps them: Clarify their goals and vision Navigate challenges and uncertainties Strengthen leadership capacity Build confidence in their change-making journey Maintain momentum throughout the program This personalized support ensures that each fellow can develop their unique pathway toward impact. 3. Two In-Person Intensive Gatherings in Switzerland The fellowship includes two immersive residential experiences held on a biodynamic farm in Switzerland. These in-person intensives provide opportunities to: Build meaningful relationships with fellow participants Engage in hands-on learning experiences Deepen personal reflection and leadership development Explore regenerative and biodynamic agricultural practices Strengthen collaborative project work Connect theory with practice in a real-world setting The residential format creates a powerful environment for community-building and transformative learning. 4. Access to a Global Community Fellows become part of an international network of: Mentors Practitioners Food systems innovators Project collaborators Social entrepreneurs Community leaders Alumni This global community creates opportunities for long-term partnerships, collaboration, knowledge sharing, and continued impact beyond the fellowship period. Who Should Apply? The Fellowship for Action is ideal for individuals who: Want to deepen their understanding of food systems and move toward action. Seek clarity about their role in driving food systems transformation. Have an idea, initiative, project, or intervention they want to develop further. Value personal growth alongside professional development. Believe collaboration is essential for creating systemic change. Are excited to learn from people with diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. Want to create positive impact within their organizations, communities, or regions. Applicants do not need to come from a traditional food-sector background to participate. Benefits of the FoodHaus Fellowship for Action Participants will gain access to numerous benefits, including: Leadership Development Strengthen collaborative leadership skills. Develop confidence as a changemaker. Learn how to navigate complexity and uncertainty. Food Systems Understanding Build a deeper understanding of interconnected food systems. Identify leverage points for meaningful intervention. Explore sustainable and regenerative approaches. Project Development Transform ideas into actionable initiatives. Receive structured guidance throughout the design process. Develop strategies for implementation and impact. International Network Join a diverse cohort of global changemakers. Build relationships across sectors and disciplines. Access future collaboration opportunities. Personal Growth Increase self-awareness and resilience. Clarify personal values and purpose. Develop relational skills necessary for long-term change. Diverse Backgrounds Welcome FoodHaus intentionally welcomes participants from a broad range of professional and lived experiences. Potential participants may include: Social entrepreneurs Farmers Chefs Policymakers Artists and creatives Educators Activists Scientists Community organizers Social workers Development practitioners Nonprofit professionals Public sector professionals Students and emerging leaders The fellowship values diversity of perspective and encourages applications from anyone committed to advancing a more equitable and sustainable food future. Scholarship Opportunities To increase accessibility and participation, FoodHaus is offering a limited number of scholarships for selected participants. Applicants seeking financial support should ensure they submit their applications before the scholarship deadline. Why This Fellowship Matters Food systems influence nearly every aspect of society, including health, climate resilience, economic opportunity, social justice, biodiversity, and community well-being. Addressing these interconnected challenges requires leaders who can think systemically, collaborate effectively, and take meaningful action. The FoodHaus Fellowship for Action aims to cultivate exactly these kinds of leaders by combining practical learning, personal transformation, global collaboration, and real-world impact. For individuals passionate about creating positive change through food, this fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity to develop the skills, networks, and confidence needed to move from vision to action. Click here for more and to apply Final Thoughts The FoodHaus Fellowship for Action 2026–2027 offers an exceptional platform for changemakers who believe food can be a powerful driver of social and environmental transformation. Through systems thinking, leadership development, coaching, global networking, and hands-on learning experiences in Switzerland, participants will gain the tools and support necessary to create meaningful and lasting change. If you are ready to turn your vision into action and contribute to a more just and sustainable food system, this fellowship could be the ideal next step in your leadership journey. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post FoodHaus Fellowship for Action 2026–2027: Fully Immersive 9-Month Global Fellowship Empowering Changemakers to Transform Food Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
